decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble a board of artifacts, using cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga, and then use them efficiently to overwhelm the opponent with aggro pressure. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Arcbound Ravager provide synergy with the artifact theme, while cards like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke help control the board. The deck aims to deploy its threats quickly and efficiently while disrupting the opponent's game plan with removal and disruption spells.

This deck focuses on playing cheap artifacts to take advantage of affinity for artifacts, quickly deploying threats to overwhelm the opponent. It aims to use cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga to generate mana and card advantage, while also utilizing cards like Arcbound Ravager and Kappa Cannoneer to put pressure on the opponent. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke to control the board and protect its key pieces. Overall, the deck seeks to win quickly through aggressive artifact-based strategies in the Modern format.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana and efficiently tutoring for key pieces to bring out Primeval Titan, which can then search for more lands and set up for a game-ending combo with cards like Scapeshift or Amulet of Vigor. The deck aims to create explosive turns where it can quickly overwhelm the opponent with a large number of lands entering the battlefield and creating advantageous board states. The combination of land ramp, tutors, and powerful finishers makes this deck a formidable force in the Modern format.

This deck focuses on using a combination of creatures, enchantments, and spells to put pressure on the opponent quickly. With a mix of aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with powerful enchantments like Starfield Shepherd and Witch Enchanter, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. Utilizing cards like Goblin Bombardment and Solitude for removal, the deck aims to maintain board control while pushing for a fast victory. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors for casting spells efficiently.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and removal spells. The deck's main strategy is to control the board with efficient removal like Path to Exile and Cleansing Wildfire while constantly pressuring the opponent with creatures like Solitude and White Orchid Phantom. Cards like Price of Freedom and Wrath of the Skies provide additional ways to maintain board dominance and close out the game. The deck also includes Chandra, Awakened Inferno as a powerful finisher to help secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Blade of the Bloodchief, Basking Broodscale, and Glaring Fleshraker to generate creature tokens and pump them up. It also utilizes Ancient Stirrings to search for key combo pieces like Eldrazi Temple and Urza's Saga. Malevolent Rumble and Kozilek's Command help control the board while cards like Emrakul, the Promised End provide a powerful finisher. The deck also includes some utility cards like Vexing Bauble and Boseiju, Who Endures to further support the combo strategy. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a mix of creature tokens and powerful spells.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through counterspells, removal, and hand disruption. The deck includes cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Drown in the Loch to disrupt the opponent's strategy, along with Counterspell and Force of Negation to stop key plays. Tasigur, the Golden Fang and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares provide additional threats, while Orcish Bowmasters and Psychic Frog offer creature removal options. The deck aims to maintain control of the game and outvalue the opponent through card advantage and efficient removal spells.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with cards like Utopia Sprawl and Talisman of Impulse to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker. Control elements include cards like Kozilek's Return and Malevolent Rumble to handle opposing threats, while cards like Sowing Mycospawn and Ugin's Labyrinth provide additional disruption. The deck also includes some unique Eldrazi creatures like Herigast, Erupting Nullkite and Sire of Seven Deaths for added versatility. Overall, the deck aims to control the game early and finish with powerful Eldrazi threats.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping mana to cast powerful creatures like Primeval Titan and combo pieces like Scapeshift to search for specific lands and win the game in one explosive turn. Cards like Amulet of Vigor and Arboreal Grazer help accelerate mana production, while utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Mirrorpool provide additional value. The deck aims to create a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and explosive combos, utilizing synergy between lands and spells to achieve victory.

The main concept of this Modern Control - UrzaTron deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of powerful Eldrazi creatures, colorless removal spells, and disruptive artifacts. The deck aims to assemble the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Tower, Power Plant, and Mine) to generate a large amount of mana to play devastating threats like Ugin, the Spirit Dragon and Karn Liberated. It also leverages cards like Chalice of the Void and Karn, the Great Creator to disrupt the opponent's strategy and secure victory through overwhelming board presence and control.

The main focus of this deck is to generate high amounts of mana quickly through rituals and mana sources like Manamorphose and Ruby Medallion. This mana is then used to cast high-impact spells like Glimpse the Impossible and Ral, Monsoon Mage to combo off and deal lethal damage. Past in Flames allows for recursion of key cards, while Valakut Awakening and Wish provide additional card selection and potential combo pieces. The deck aims to consistently execute its combo strategy and overwhelm the opponent with explosive spells.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly swarm the board with artifacts, using cards like Kappa Cannoneer and Mishra's Bauble to fuel the aggressive playstyle. Salvage Titan and Emry, Lurker of the Loch provide additional utility by recurring artifacts from the graveyard. The deck also includes cards like Arcbound Ravager and Urza's Saga to increase the power and flexibility of the artifact creatures. Engineered Explosives and Pithing Needle offer control options, while Tormod's Crypt and Welding Jar provide graveyard and protection support. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient artifact-focused strategy.

This deck focuses on assembling the Amulet Titan combo as quickly as possible, utilizing cards like Amulet of Vigor, Primeval Titan, and Urza's Saga to generate mana and search for key pieces. By ramping quickly and efficiently, the deck can overwhelm opponents with powerful combo plays, such as using Scapeshift to search for lands that can trigger multiple times with Amulet of Vigor. The deck also includes utility lands like Gruul Turf and Boseiju, Who Endures to support its game plan and protect against disruption from opponents.

The deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence through the use of artifact creatures and synergies with artifacts. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient threats and card advantage provided by cards like Thoughtcast and Mishra's Bauble. Urza's Saga is a key card in the deck, allowing for the generation of powerful artifact tokens and tutoring for essential pieces. The inclusion of Claws of Gix and Tormod's Crypt provides utility and disruption against opposing strategies. Overall, the deck is a fast-paced and aggressive build that leverages the strength of artifacts in Modern.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive artifact creatures and efficient spells to disrupt their game plan. By utilizing artifacts like Engineered Explosives and Mox Opal, along with cards that can be played for free like Mishra's Bauble, the deck aims to establish a strong board presence early on. With the ability to generate mana and card advantage, along with the potential to control the opponent's options, this deck can put pressure on the opponent and dictate the pace of the game.

The goal of this deck is to use a variety of creature toolbox cards to set up combos and generate value. With a mix of sacrifice outlets, tutors, and recursive creatures, the deck aims to create synergies and generate incremental advantage over the opponent. By utilizing cards like Chord of Calling, Cauldron Familiar, and Carrion Feeder, the deck can assemble powerful combinations to control the board and win the game. The mana base is designed to support the toolbox strategy by providing consistent access to the necessary colors and lands.
